name: Pecan Pralines- Patout
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter a medium saucepan to make cleaning easier later.
2. Pour in the heavy cream and place over high heat.
3. When it begins to boil, add the raw or brown sugar and stir rapidly until it dissolves completely.
4. Stir in the vanilla and pecans, then continue cooking over medium heat while stirring frequently until the mixture reaches the soft-ball stage (236°F on a candy thermometer).
5. Remove from heat and quickly beat in the butter. This helps stop the cooking process.
6. The candy will lose its glossy appearance and become very cloudy.
7. Lay out a long strip of waxed paper on a work surface.
8. Moisten the waxed paper with a damp towel.
9. Drop spoonfuls of the hot praline mixture onto the waxed paper, stirring occasionally to keep it well combined.
10. Remove the pralines from the paper before they have cooled completely; later it will be difficult to remove them without breaking.
11. Store in tightly sealed containers with layers of waxed paper between each layer.
Makes about 2 dozen
From Alex Patout's Cajun Home Cooking
